description: Unlike previous emperors, who were clean shaven, Hadrian (reigned 117–38 CE) wore a beard, perhaps in emulation of the Greek philosophers he revered. Here, Hadrian’s closely cropped beard contrasts with the luxurious curls that frame his face. This sculpture also features an innovative trend in Roman portraiture: the artist carefully carved the irises and pupils of the eyes rather than conventionally rendering them in paint.
title: Portrait Head of Emperor Hadrian
